title: nie-daemon crate: Cargo.toml + axum server skeleton
description:
**What:** Create the nie-daemon binary crate with a minimal axum health endpoint, add it to the workspace, and add tower-http to workspace dependencies.

**Algorithm:**
1. Create directory daemon/ in workspace root
2. Write daemon/Cargo.toml: name=nie-daemon, edition=2021; deps: nie-core.workspace, axum.workspace (features=[ws]), tokio.workspace, tower-http={workspace=true, features=[cors,validate-request]}, serde.workspace, serde_json.workspace, anyhow.workspace, tracing.workspace, tracing-subscriber.workspace
3. Add tower-http = { version = '0.6', features = [] } to [workspace.dependencies] in root Cargo.toml
4. Write daemon/src/main.rs: #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()>; bind TcpListener to 127.0.0.1:7734; single route GET /health returning 'ok'; axum::serve; return Ok(())
5. Write daemon/src/lib.rs: empty for now (pub mod declarations added in later beads)
6. Add 'daemon' to workspace.members in root Cargo.toml
7. Run cargo build -p nie-daemon to verify compilation

**Defensive programming rules:**
- Default address is 127.0.0.1:7734 hardcoded in main() — never 0.0.0.0
- No unwrap() without comment in production paths
- Keyfile and sensitive data not touched in this bead (pure scaffold)

**Agent team:** Single Implementer (R not needed — no existing code to read, just create new). Validator after.

**Tests:**
- test_health: cargo build -p nie-daemon exits 0 (compilation oracle)
- After build: curl http://127.0.0.1:7734/health → 200 'ok'
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-daemon exits 0; cargo fmt --all exits 0; cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings exits 0
close_reason: crate scaffold created, cargo build passes
